summary: Cathy O'Neil announces her participation in two panels at the Left Forum conference at Pace University. The first panel is a debate with Doug Henwood about shrinking the financial sector, moderated by economist Suresh Naidu. The second panel is a public meeting of the Occupy Wall Street Alternative Banking group, focused on breaking up megabanks and demonstrating how the group operates. She encourages readers to register and attend.
key_points: O'Neil will debate strategies for shrinking the financial sector with Wall Street author Doug Henwood
The Alternative Banking group meeting will demonstrate their participatory discussion format and get audience members 'on stack'
The panels focus on breaking up megabanks as a concrete policy goal
The Left Forum brings together progressive thinkers including Noam Chomsky
money_quotes: I'll be debating how to shrink the financial sector with Doug Henwood
The idea is to explain to the panel audience how we roll in Alt Banking
We expect them to enjoy getting on stack
key_insight: Progressive organizing around financial reform involves both intellectual debate about policy and participatory grassroots meetings that model democratic engagement.
